slides.title: LoRA: Low-Rank Adaptation of Large Language Models
slides.subtitle: Paper Deep-Read

slides.label: Trainable Parameters per Task
slides.desc: GPT-3 full fine-tuning: each downstream task requires a complete 175B parameter copy. N tasks = N × 175B in storage.

slides.label: Prior Art
slides.heading: Why Existing Efficient Methods Fall Short

items.title: Adapter Layers
items.desc: Add model depth → inference latency, especially on short sequences

items.title: Prefix/Prompt Tuning
items.desc: Consume input length → compress usable sequence

items.title: Common Issue
items.desc: Rarely match full fine-tuning baseline — efficiency vs quality tradeoff

slides.label: Core Insight
slides.heading: Weight Updates Are Low-Rank
points: Over-parameterized models live on a low-dimensional manifold
Hypothesis: adaptation ΔW also has low intrinsic rank
Even with d = 12,288, rank r = 1–2 suffices
Freeze W₀, train only A and B matrices

slides.label: Method
slides.heading: LoRA: Freeze W₀, Inject Low-Rank BA

steps.title: Train
steps.desc: W₀ frozen, only train A and B

steps.title: Merge
steps.desc: Deploy: W = W₀ + BA — same as original model

columns.title: Efficiency
points: No gradient storage for frozen params

slides.label: GLUE Results
slides.heading: GLUE Benchmark: Fewer Params, Same or Better

slides.label: GPT-3 Results
slides.heading: GPT-3 175B: Still Holds at Scale
slides.desc: WikiSQL, MNLI, SAMSum all match or exceed full fine-tuning.

slides.label: Rank Analysis
slides.heading: How Much Rank Do You Need?
slides.desc: Adapting Wq + Wv: rank r=1 already sufficient; r=64 adds minimal gain.

slides.text: Low-rank adaptation = parameter efficient + zero extra inference latency + quality on par or better. One foundation model, many hot-swappable LoRA modules.
